mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and worsening economic conditions, leading to flight-to-safety flows into Treasuries and lower 10-year yields over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE continues to rise but 10-year yield either rises or remains unchanged over 20 trading days in at least 3 consecutive instances, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falling below the 100 level indicates significant contraction in manufacturing activity, which reinforces recession fears and depresses yields as investors seek safe-haven assets.
failure_condition: If INDPRO falls below 100 and within 20 trading days the 10-year yield increases by more than 10 basis points, or if no decline in yield is observed in more than 50% of such events, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate simultaneous demand and supply shocks, deepening the recession and driving 10-year yields lower as risk appetite collapses.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and INDPRO show month-over-month declines but the 10-year yield rises by more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days, this combined signal is invalidated. Accept if yield declines in at least 60% of such paired events.

mechanism_narrative: The relationship between housing starts and 10-year yields is regime-dependent: during housing downturns (starts below their 12-month moving average), further declines in starts signal economic weakness and lead to lower yields; but once starts recover above the moving average, improvements signal strength and lead to higher yields. Currently in the downturn phase, the directional prediction is negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: If HOUST declines further but 10-year yield rises by more than 20bp over 60 trading days, or if HOUST recovers above MA but yields fall, the regime-conditional hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) reduces inflation expectations and nominal growth prospects, putting downward pressure on long-term Treasury yields over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL declines by more than 0.5 points but 10-year yield increases by more than 10 basis points over 60 trading days, or if the rank correlation between CPI changes and yield changes is not negative over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 500,000 triggers immediate recession anxiety, prompting investors to rush into Treasuries and pushing yields lower within a week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 500,000 in a single month
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines by more than 500k and 10-year yield does not fall by at least 5bp within 5 trading days, or if the average yield change in such events is positive, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of the federal funds rate being at its effective lower bound (0.65%) and rapidly rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a deep recession with limited monetary policy room, reinforcing a deflationary outlook and pushing yields lower through both flight-to-safety and reduced growth expectations.
regime_hint: zero lower bound and labor market collapse
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains low (below 0.75%) and UNRATE rises, but 10-year yield increases by more than 10bp over 20 trading days in more than one instance, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Falling retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflect collapsing consumer demand, which depresses economic activity and inflation expectations, leading to lower 10-year yields over short and medium horizons.
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines month-over-month but 10-year yield does not decline within 5 trading days in at least 70% of such occurrences, the hypothesis is invalidated.
